expenses in the books of accounts.

Reclassification of	Food
Expenses to Representation Expenses will be done during the next budget year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management		agreed to segregate the cost and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the		road		sub- components	of	 the road	networks,	and compute			the depreciation of each sub-component separately, except for the		road			lot component.

observations.reason: Management reasoned	out that	they had difficulty	in complying with the recommenda- tion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and it was agreed that the Property Officer:
Prepare	and	duly accomplish		the
Inventory		and Acceptance Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Report		on		Waste Materials (RWM), with all		the		required
documents	and
information,and submit to the DAC for their	appropriate action.

Management was not able to fully	comply
with	the
recommen- dation.
observations.reason: One	 of	the unserviceable vehicles	was used	for	the cable		tramline at Barangay O- ong, while the other car was still	at		the shaded area at the back of the municipal compound.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.

An appeal with the	COA Central Office for		the

disallowed purchase   of

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management		agreed to review the viability of		all			 the unimplemented projects, through the Municipal Development Council and	the		MDRRM Council, for possible reversion	and		 re- appropriation to other development		and MDRRM projects that are more feasible and responsive	 to	 the needs of the general public and address the potential risks during
calamities.
observations.managementAction: Some of		the unimplemented projects		were re-aligned		to other development projects.
observations.reason: Evaluation	of other unimplemented projects	was not	conducted in CY 2024 but no reason was given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management source out funds to assist the association in making sure that the customized rice mill machine is properly assembled	and functioning in order for the community to benefit from the economic value that can be derived from using the machine.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason:
Lack of interest among		the members of the Farmer’s Association		to restore		the machine.		The operation of the Rice Mill is not feasible anymore because		the rice fields	were converted		to
gardens	for planting	crops
other than rice.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and it was agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the stale checks after proper verification in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the Municipality’s books of accounts and henceforth, ensure that appropriate adjusting and reversing entries are promptly prepared to cancel stale checks; and
The Municipal Treasurer give notices to payees at least one month before their checks become stale to avoid the same deficiency in the future.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit a written justification/explanation why the expenditure be not disallowed in audit; and
Henceforth, ensure that a Supplemental Budget, duly supported with a revised AIP or SIP, be passed before incurring any expenditures out of savings/unexpended appropriations, except for augmentation which is through a mere Appropriation Ordinance.

We recommended and Management agreed to review the feasibility and necessity of the unimplemented PPAs, then amend, if necessary, in accordance with procedural guidelines; thereafter, utilize the unexpended balances of the capital outlay by providing DRRM activities to be implemented in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure the following:
The Identified PPAs for DRRM are first included in the LDRRMFP before these are incorporated in the AIP/SIP pursuant to the BOM for LGUs; and
The PPAs in the enabling AO or Budget originate from the AIP/SIP; thus, project descriptions are the same.

We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Require the project coordinator/designated PESO to submit written explanation/justification on the payment to an individual who was not a qualified TUPAD beneficiary and without an approved contract of service and DTR, and on the discrepancies/inconsistencies of the names and signatures of the 125 TUPAD beneficiaries;
Submit the duly signed and approved DTRs or Daily Attendance Sheets of all the TUPAD beneficiaries/recipients and the TUPAD Completion Report; and
Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the guidelines embodied under DOLE DO No. 239, including the use of appropriate forms and documents as provided in its annexes, in order to avoid incurring irregular expenditures in the implementation of future TUPAD programs.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that:
The	Municipal Engineer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the SWAs of the projects listed in Table 1, and the duly signed Payroll to support the reimbursement    of
₱8,000.00;



The	payment	for wages		of		laborers through reimbursements	 be stopped;





The	Municipal Accountant reclassify the CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts of the completed projects based on the SWA submitted by the Municipal Engineering Office and provide for the corresponding depreciation; and

Henceforth,	the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the SWA for all projects as supporting
documents	for payment, whichever mode of procurement is used.
observations.managementAction:
The	MEO
furnished	the Municipal Accountant with copies of SWA for		all
completed projects identified	and the	liquidation payroll.

The	office stopped		all
payments	of laborers through reimbursements by	the
Municipal Engineer as of May 24,2024.
The	four
completed projects	were
classified	as
PPE	in	the books.


Copies of SWA for	all
completed projects in CY 2024	were
submitted to the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
The	Municipal Accounting    Office
recognize income upon collection of other taxes, fees, charges and other revenues for fair presentation	of
income   in   the
Statement	of Operations; and
The	Municipal Treasury		Office observe cut-off and timely turnover of the RCDs to the Accounting Office for recording especially at yearend.
observations.managementAction: Management complied	with the recommen- dation.
observations.reason: Strict observance	of the cut-off and timely turnover of RCDs to the Accounting Office	was observed/ enforced.

observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee was created.


Physical count of	the
inventories were conducted for the medicines at the Municipal Health   Office
but no RPCI was submitted.
observations.reason: No reasons given for the partial physical count.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
a) The	Municipal Accountant record the purchases of supplies	and materials, drugs and medicines, and diesel fuel to be carried in stock as inventory regardless of whether
or  not  they  are consumed within the accounting period, compute	and
recognize		the corresponding expenses	for	actual issuances to the end- users and the value of inventories using the perpetual	inventory method;
The Provision of adequate stockroom that   is   properly
enclosed	and protected from access of	unauthorized personnel to store all procured items of the Municipality, including appropriate storage tank for fuel and oil and assignment of a qualified personnel to take charge of the safekeeping, issuance and control of the inventories; and
The preparation and maintenance of the Requisition	and Issuance Slip, Report on Supplies and Materials    Issued,
Supplies	Ledger Cards and Stock Cards by the offices
and	personnel concerned.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied	with the recommen- dation.





Not Implemented





Not Implemented



Management did not comply with	the
recommen- dation.





No action taken by Management.


Each department has their supplies in their offices.


There was no centralized stockroom	for the supplies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
The	Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) report and remit in full their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer pursuant to Sec. 7 of COA Circular No. 92- 382; and
The	Municipal Treasurer		and
Liquidating Officer thoroughly review the Report of Collection and Deposits (RCDs) submitted by the Revenue Collection Clerks by verifying the amount of collections against the actual accountable   forms
issued.
observations.managementAction:
The RCCs remitted in full all collections but not on a daily basis.



Collections were	verified against		the Official Receipts issued.
observations.reason: Collections were	remitted in	full	on	a weekly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:

Submission of the duly filled up DTTs and Gas Slips for the transactions in Annex A; and
Henceforth all the required information be indicated in the DTTs and Gas Slips by the personnel concerned. Also, the DTTs be prepared per official  travel  and
without alterations after their approval.
observations.managementAction:
No duly filled up DTTs and Gas Slips were submitted.

Management complied	with the recommen- dation.
observations.reason: No	reasons given.

observations.recommendation:
We   recommended
and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that the required warranty security for purchases of goods be imposed to assure that manufacturing defects
may be corrected.
observations.managementAction: Warranty securities	for purchased goods	were imposed		 by Management.

observations.recommendation:
We	recommended	and management agreed to:

Hire a reasonable number of qualified engineers to help in the preparation of plans and program of works and in project monitoring;

Consider		funding significant		projects and re-evaluate the unimplemented projects considering the	rising	cost	of construction
materials.
observations.managementAction:
Engineers were hired on job order basis in CY 2024.



No evaluation was done in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Management overlooked	to evaluate	the unimplemented projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the GFPS of the Municipality:

Prepare the GAD Agenda as basis for the identification of PPAs to be included in the annual GPB; and

Henceforth, submit to the DILG for review the adjusted GPB for any changes in the original	GPB pursuant to Section 6 of PCW-DILG-DBM- NEDA JMC No.
2016-01.
observations.managementAction:
GAD	Agenda was prepared.


Management complied	with the recommen- dation.

publication	and posting process.
observations.managementAction: Management hired/appointed a	Municipal Treasurer on a permanent status.

and/administration of the HGDG.

Training   on
HGDG was conducted in August 2024.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Property Officer:

Prepare and duly accomplish the Inventory and Acceptance Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Report on Waste Materials (RWM), with all the required documents and information, and submit to the DAC for their appropriate action; and 

Secure and provide a shed for the motor vehicles to deter stealing of the vehicles’ parts and avoid further deterioration pending disposal.
observations.managementAction:
No action.

The parking area of the unserviceable vehicle was shaded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the GFPS of the Municipality:

Prepare the GAD Agenda as basis for the identification of PPAs to be included in the annual GPB; and 

Henceforth, submit to the DILG for review the adjusted GPB for any changes in the original GPB pursuant to Section 6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

No GAD Agenda was prepared.

No action.

The Agency is not equipped with the knowledge and skills in the formulation of a GAD Agenda.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management started to deduct from the payroll of those connected with the Agency in October 2022.
observations.reason: Management filed an appeal with the COA Central Office for the disallowed PEI. No settlement on the  disallowed purchase of  foliar fertilizer since it is under litigation by the court.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to review the viability of all the unimplemented projects, through the Municipal Development Council and the MDRRM Council, for possible reversion and re-appropriation to other development and MDRRM projects that are more feasible and responsive to the needs of the general public and address the potential risks during calamities.
observations.managementAction:
Some of the unimplemented projects were reverted and re-appropriated.

The review of the unimplemented projects is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management consider the following:

Fill-up the vacant position with the most qualified personnel through a wide reach of publication and posting process.
observations.managementAction:
The Treasury Office is still headed by  an Acting Municipal Treasurer.

Management published the hiring for the vacant position several times in CY 2022.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified applicants despite several publications.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: Training on HGDG was conducted in CY 2022.
observations.reason: Despite the training, they still lack adequate skills/ knowledge to administer HGDG on the GAD PPAs. The Agency still needs training for them to apply the HGDG tool.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management source out funds to assist the association in making sure that the customized rice mill machine is properly assembled and functioning in order for the community to benefit from the economic value that can be derived from using the machine.
observations.managementAction: The machine was assembled and installed but not functional. The Agency planned to revive the Association if they are still interested.
observations.reason: Lack of funds needed to make the project operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the Accountable Forms with Money Value totaling ₱28,520.00; and henceforth, ensure the proper classification of expenses in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed ensure the following:
The Municipal Accounting Office recognize income upon collection of other taxes, fees, charges and other revenues for fair presentation of income in the Statement of Operations; and 
The Municipal Treasury Office observe cut-off and timely turnover of the RCDs to the Accounting Office for recording especially at yearend.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
The Inventory Committee of the Municipality be created to take charge in the physical count of inventories;
The physical count of inventories be conducted by the Inventory Committee semestrally and that the RPCI is prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office and the Audit Team; and
The Accounting Office reconcile the RPCI with their accounting records and draw the appropriate adjustments for any discrepancies noted after proper verification.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following: 
The Municipal Accountant record the purchases of supplies and materials, drugs and medicines, and diesel fuel to be carried in stock as inventory regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, compute and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the end-users and the value of inventories using the perpetual inventory method; 
The Provision of adequate stockroom that is properly enclosed and protected from access of unauthorized personnel to store all procured items of the Municipality, including appropriate storage tank for fuel and oil and assignment of a qualified personnel to take charge of the safekeeping, issuance and control of the inventories; and
The preparation and maintenance of the Requisition and Issuance Slip, Report on Supplies and Materials Issued, Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards by the offices and personnel concerned.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
The Revenue Collection Clerks report and remit in full their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer pursuant to Sec. 7 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and
The Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer thoroughly review the RCDs submitted by the Revenue Collection Clerks by verifying the amount of collections against the actual accountable forms issued.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the following:
Submission of the duly filled up DTTs and Gas Slips for the transactions in Annex A; and
Henceforth all the required information be indicated in the DTTs and Gas Slips by the personnel concerned. Also, the DTTs be prepared per official travel and without alterations after their approval.
We recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure that the required warranty security for purchases of goods be imposed   to assure that manufacturing defects may be corrected.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue enforcing the settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
 
Drop from the books of accounts of the Municipality those PPEs that were turned over or under the control of other agencies and organizations;  

Draw the appropriate adjusting entries to reverse the depreciation of the road opening projects and unfinished buildings, and reclassify and correct the depreciation of the misclassified PPE accounts taking into account the summary of misstatements provided in Table 4; and 

Segregate the cost and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road sub-components of the road networks, and compute the depreciation of each sub-component separately, except for the road lot component.
observations.managementAction:
Appropriate adjustments were made on the deficiencies.

The suggested adjusting entries were adopted and booked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revert the appropriation for the rescue vehicle from the Special Trust Fund to the General Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate adjustments were made on the deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the following entry to adjust the unrecorded expenses and overstatement of the cash advance for payroll in the books of accounts.

Henceforth, to ensure that all expenditures incurred are recorded and properly classified in the books of accounts in order to reflect the true financial position and performance in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate adjustments were made on the deficiencies.
observations.reason: Misclassification of Food Supplies Expenses

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to, henceforth, conduct a thorough review of all the journal entries prepared by his staff before these are approved or entered in the books of accounts in order to avoid errors and ensure the correct and proper presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:

Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the current year and uncollected RPT-Basic/SET of the prior years based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and 

Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to set up the correct RPT-Basic/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT-Basic/SET Income in CY 2023 based on the amount certified by the Municipal Treasurer for the current year and uncollected RPT of the prior years. For the succeeding years, only the amount of RPT Receivable for the current year shall be set up.
observations.managementAction:
Detailed RPT Receivables were prepared.

Detailed RPT receivables were booked.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to: 

Conduct a more aggressive tax collection and information campaign in each Barangay; 

Prepare the Notice of Delinquency and comply with the posting requirement. If possible, send individual Notice of Delinquency to the concerned taxpayers; and  

Fast track the implementation of the general tax revision of all real property in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Treasury conducted IEC in the barangays.

Notices of Delinquency were sent to delinquent taxpayers.

General tax revision was done in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, as the Local Chief Executive and key official of the Municipality, agreed to:

Ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), in coordination with other offices concerned, formulate the CLUP of the Municipality following the procedures set by the Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development (DHSUD).  Conduct research on other LGUs that have successfully prepared their CLUPs as inputs or benchmark for the preparation of the Municipality’s CLUP; 

Request for additional funding from the Sangguniang Bayan for the hiring of a consultant or an expert to help the MPDO in formulating the CLUP if necessary; and    

 Submit the CLUP to the Sangguniang Bayan for their appropriate action.
observations.managementAction:
The MPDO drafted the CLUP of the municipality.

The Agency sought the help of DHSUD who in turn assisted them in the formulation of the CLUP.

The needed documents from SB were complied. Awaiting for the approved and final copy of the CLUP.

The Municipality is always finding ways in the implementation of its infrastructure projects.

The Municipality will be hiring two additional engineers.

The Municipality is contracting Deed of Donations, where projects involved are lots.

The Agency already considered re-evaluating the long time unimplemented projects.
observations.reason:
No engineers were hired in CY 2023.

No re-evaluation in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the BAC agreed to require its Secretariat to publish the NOA, Contract, and NTP in the PhilGEPS or in the agency website. 

We further recommended that they shall keep themselves abreast on how to access and use the PhilGEPS or to create/install a website of the municipality, by seeking assistance from the BAC Secretariat of other LGUs and government agencies that have successfully accessed and published the required documents in the PhilGEPS or in their agency website, among others.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC have published the NOAs, Contracts, and NTPs in the PhilGEPS.

The Agency is using the PHILGEPS in its publication.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to strictly enforce compliance with the required timelines in the issuance of NOA and NTP in order not to delay the execution of projects.
observations.managementAction: The Agency has a timeline checklist for the procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the concerned offices to submit the distribution sheets duly signed by the recipients showing the quantity of goods received for review by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Agency  complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit a certification  from  the concerned payees and justification of management relative to the deficiencies noted in paragraphs 3.4(d &f) and 3.4 (e & g), respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management enforced the settlement and recipients were able to fully pay their accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to issue separate resolutions recommending the mode of procurement and award of contract.
observations.managementAction: Management  fully complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to draw    the      appropriate adjusting entry to remove the estimated administrative and other overhead costs from the “Infrastructure Asset” and “Other Payables” accounts.
observations.managementAction: The administrative overhead was excluded from the infrastructure assets for CY 2019 onwards

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to submit written explanations or justifications on the posting of a Request for Quotation when there was already a publication for open competitive public bidding for the construction of Pikdol-Paghok Pathway; the conflicting dates in the NOA, NTP, SWA and Performance Bond; and the excessive Performance Bond.
observations.managementAction: Written explanation was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to deduct from the salaries and any money due the persons liable the audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: There were partial settlements in CY 2019 but halted since then. Salary deductions resumed in October 2023.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management appropriate funds to replace the stolen materials for the project to be operational. Upon replacement, turnover the project to the incumbent Punong Barangay for custody and maintenance. Further, educate the constituents to value and safeguard projects given to them which could help uplift their living condition.
observations.managementAction: Management allocated an amount in its supplemental budget and used the fund to address the deficiency. The street light is already functional.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management exert effort to coordinate with the appropriate government agencies to hasten the reclassification of the vehicle from private to government plate.
observations.managementAction: Vehicle is unserviceable.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to require the:

Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate income account in future similar receipts wherein the purpose is not very specific and it cannot be classified as a trust liability; 

Municipal  Accountant  to record  the   inventory  account  upon  purchase  of goods for distribution or consumption and recognize the expense after issuance/distribution pursuant to Sections 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs; and 

the concerned offices to submit the distribution sheets duly signed by the recipients showing the quantity of goods received for review by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management submit the following: 

- a certification  from  the concerned payees and justification of management relative to the deficiencies noted in paragraphs 3.4(d &f) and 3.4 (e & g), respectively;

-  lacking  documents  to  this Office such as copy of the duly accomplished SAC forms, photocopy of the identification card with signatures of the beneficiaries, photo of all the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the cash subsidy; and certification from the Barangay that the beneficiaries are in need of cash assistance due to Covid 19; and 

- other relevant documents to support the explanations/justifications.
observations.managementAction: The needed/lacking documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to submit written explanations or justifications on the posting of a Request for Quotation when there was already a publication for open competitive public bidding for the construction of Pikdol-Paghok Pathway; the conflicting dates in the NOA, NTP, SWA and Performance Bond; and the excessive Performance Bond.
observations.managementAction: A written  explanation has not  been submitted but the BAC chairperson promised to submit his justification soon.

observations.recommendation:
1.4  We have recommended and Management agreed to require:
	the Municipal Accountant to:
draw the appropriate journal entry vouchers to record the infrastructure assets and corresponding liability account for the above completed projects;
determine and record all completed projects at year-end, whether paid or unpaid, based on duly approved Statement of Work Accomplished and Inspection Reports from the Municipal Engineering Office.
drop from the PPE accounts the properties costing less than P15,000.00;
record all properties costing less than P15,000.00 as inventory upon acquisition and expense upon issuance of the ICS; and
the Property Custodian to replace the existing Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) issued to the end users with the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS).

2.4  We have recommended and management agreed to:
require the Municipal Accountant and the concerned heads of the implementing department/offices to thoroughly verify/validate the status of implementation of the pertinent projects/programs with unused balances; and
return the unutilized funds of completed and discontinued projects/programs to the funding agencies after proper verification/validation.

3.4  We have recommended and management agreed to require the contractors to post the required warranty security upon release of the Retention Money and final acceptance of the completed projects pursuant to Section 62.2.3.3, IRR of  RA No. 9184.

4.5  We have recommended and management agreed to amend its policy on the rate of Retention Money from 5% to 10% in compliance with the IRR of RA No. 9184. Also, apply the rate of Retention Money on the contract cost prior to deduction of the liquidated damage.
5.4 We have recommended and management agreed to strictly enforce compliance with the required timelines in the issuance of NOA and NTP in order not to delay the execution of projects.

6.4  We have recommended and management agreed to require:
the Municipal Budget Officer to review the prior years’ entries in the Registry of Appropriations-Continuing Appropriations, taking into account the estimated income as against the actual realized  income from CY 2018 and prior years, and make the appropriate adjustments for  any errors and omissions noted,; and
the Municipal Accountant to check and determine if all the prior years’ (CY 2018 & below) 20% DF allocations were transferred from the General Fund account to the 20% DF bank account.

7.8  We have recommended and management agreed to:
ascertain and re-appropriate the balances of completed projects under the 20%DF and MDRRMF, including the unspent EAO which was recorded in the Other Payables account, taking into account the effect of  the preceding observation. The unspent EAO of completed projects out of trust funds may be appropriated for Covid 19 related expenditures; and
review the viability of all the unimplemented projects, through the Municipal Development Council and the MDRRM Council,  for possible reversion and re-appropriation to other development and MDRRM projects that are more feasible  and responsive to the needs of the general public and address the potential risks during calamities.
We have recommended that management should continue enforcing  the settlement of the disallowances.

require the BAC to issue a resolution recommending award of contract to be approved by the LCE prior to contract signing or issuance of Purchase Order.




issue separate resolutions recommending the mode of procurement and award of contract;





publish in the PhilGEPS the Notice of Award, approved Contract, and Notice to  Proceed pursuant to Section 37 of the IRR, RA 9184; and 

require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to issue checks in the name  of the suppliers/creditors instead of paying through cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.managementAction: Management had made partial deductions from the salaries of liable persons.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to:

draw    the      appropriate adjusting entry to remove the estimated administrative and other overhead costs from the “Infrastructure Asset” and “Other Payables” accounts.

observations.recommendation:
2.3  We have recommended and management agreed to require the:
a)   Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate income account in future similar receipts wherein the purpose is not very specific and it cannot be classified as a trust liability;
b)   Municipal  Accountant  to record  the   inventory  account  upon  purchase  of goods for distribution or consumption and recognize the expense after issuance/distribution pursuant to Sections 188 and 190 of the GAM for LGUs; and
c)   the concerned offices to submit the distribution sheets duly signed by the recipients showing the quantity of goods received for review by the Audit Team.

3.17  We have recommended that management submit the following:
a) a certification from the concerned payees and justification of management relative to the deficiencies noted in paragraphs 3.4(d &f) and 3.4 (e & g), respectively;
b) lacking documents to this Office such as copy of the duly accomplished SAC forms, photocopy of the identification card with signatures of the beneficiaries, photo of all the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the cash subsidy; and certification from the Barangay that the beneficiaries are in need of cash assistance due to Covid 19; and
c) other relevant documents to support the explanations/justifications.

4.4 We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to relieve the Acting Municipal Treasurer from her BAC functions in compliance with DOF Order No. 042-2019 and designate another qualified official in her instead.

6.2   We have recommended and management agreed to:
a) require the BAC to issue a resolution recommending award of contract to be approved by the LCE prior to contract signing or issuance of Purchase Order.

b) issue separate resolutions recommending the mode of procurement and award of contract;

c) publish in the PhilGEPS the Notice of Award, approved Contract, and Notice to  Proceed pursuant to Section 37 of the IRR, RA 9184; and 

require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to issue checks in the name  of the suppliers/creditors instead of paying through cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.managementAction: Payables were properly classified and recorded.
The disallowance was already recorded in the books.
The liquidation was recorded in the books.
Purchases of supplies and material were first recorded as inventories. The perpetual inventory method was implemented.
Management issued demand letters to liable persons and started deducting from the salaries of concerned personnel.
The Municipal Accountant is still determining all the details of the variance and has effected some adjustments.
At present, an Acting Municipal Treasurer was designated by the DOF. The vacant position was advertised but there were no interested applicants.
Management administered the HGDG test on its GAD PPAs. Management is looking for a training program on HGDG.
Management partially implemented the recommendation. Several 20% Development projects were not implemented.
Corresponding additional pavement were constructed.
Corresponding actions taken: Acting OIC reassigned and a permanent Municipal Accountant was appointed. Management is now following the guidelines on designation of personnel.
Management was able to fully reconcile the negative balance of the Cash Local Treasury account.
The LGU has already retrieved the stolen materials but were not yet installed because there was a problem on who will shoulder the electric bill of the said project.
Partial settlements of the disallowed PEI through salary deduction were made but it was suspended as management intends to appeal.
Management started deducting from the salaries of the persons liable. There was partial refund of transportation allowance after it was disallowed under COA decisions.
Management exerted efforts to have the vehicle registered to government plate but still to no avail.

observations.recommendation: 1.5. We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly observe the proper account classification in recording transactions and to adjust the affected liability accounts for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

2.5. We have recommended and Management agreed:
a. to book-up ND No. 2015-001-101(14) amounting to ₱441,215.50 in the books of accounts to reflect correct amount of the Receivable – Disallowances/Charges account; and
b. to deduct from the salaries and any money due the persons liable the audit disallowances.

3.5. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the accumulated funds due the different barangays totaling ₱54,261.40 and to observe quarterly transfer thereafter in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
4.7. We have recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to record the liquidation made by the Barangay for the accurate presentation of the Due to LGU account and the financial statements.

5.9. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the GSO-designate and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the recording and maintenance of records and reports as required in PPSAS 12 and NGAS Volume I on supplies & materials;
b. require the GSO-designate to prepare and maintain the forms and reports for the receipt and issuance of supplies as provided in the NGAS Manual, Volumes I and II.
c. require the Municipal Accountant to observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories under all funds of the Municipality, pursuant to Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, and to classify Inventories as required in PPSAS No. 12.
6.2. We have recommended that Management issue demand letters to identified persons liable including those separated from the LGU service, and continue efforts to collect unsettled disallowances.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant will record all PPE acquired out of trust funds in the current and prior years in order to recognize the assets funded by other government agencies. Henceforth, follow the required accounting for PPE so that these are recognized in the books for accountability purposes and for fairness of presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Accountant has fully recorded all completed projects from trust funds to the proper PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Determine the details of the variance amounting to ₱2,647,452.84; b. Effect immediately the necessary adjustments to bring the balances into agreement; c. Henceforth, record simultaneously transactions to the receivable and payable accounts; and d. Perform periodic reconciliation of the Due From/To Other Funds accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management: a. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the loans being availed of by the employees, by approving loan application up to the limit of their capacity to pay, taking into consideration that their net monthly take home pay should not be less than ₱4,000.00 as prescribed in the General Appropriations Act of 2017; and b. Direct concerned employees to coordinate with the lending associations for a direct payment arrangement.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC- Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation.
Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. Administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of GPB and AR, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; and c. If necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the recommendation.
Management is on the process of administering HGDG test on its GAD PPAs.
Management is looking for a training program on HGDG.

observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. Observe the sixteen (16)spans of concrete pavement with minor scaling and furnish the Audit Team with the reports of action taken by management. If liability deterioration occurs within the defects liability period, require the contractor immediately remove and replace the defective pavement at his own expense; and b. Remove and replace the three (3) spans with transverse crack and weakened plane joint/contraction joint and the seven (7) spans with major scaling amounting to ₱99,730.22.
observations.managementAction:
Management informed the Audit Team that the minor defects did not progress after the defects period.
Management requested that instead of removing and replacing the defective portions they prefer to add new spans to make the road longer. The new spans have already been constructed and are subject for inspection.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to consider the following measures: a. Require officials/employees concerned with unliquidated cash advances to immediately settle their accounts through submission of liquidation documents and to refund any excess, otherwise, withholding of salary and/or other sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; b. Suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and other officials/employees until their previous cash advance have been liquidated, to prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances; and c. Strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management fully complied with the recommendation.
Management fully complied with the recommendation.
Management fully complied with the recommendation.

3.4. We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the loans being availed of by the employees, by approving loan applications up to the limit of their capacity to pay, taking into consideration that their net monthly take home pay should not be less than Php4,000.00 as prescribed in the General Appropriations Act of 2017; and

6.4. We have recommended and Management agreed to enforce the settlement of disallowances.

7.8. We have recommended and management agreed to:
a. clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that would support the gender issues identified in the GPB, especially if the PPA is intended to be a GAD-focused activity taking 100% of its allocation from GAD;
b. administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of GPB and AR, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; and
c. if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.

We have recommended and Management agreed to consider the following measures:
a. Require officials/ employees concerned with unliquidated cash advances to immediately settle their concerned accounts through submission of liquidation documents and to refund any excess, otherwise, withholding of salary and/or other sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and other officials/employees until their previous cash advance have been liquidated, to prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances; and
c. Strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management verbally demanded officials and employees to settle unliquidated cash advances. A minimal number of employees were not able to liquidate their cash advances. Management partially implemented the recommendation.
observations.reason: A minimal number of employees were not able to liquidate their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management promptly act and expedite the enactment of an ordinance for the creation of the MDRRMO, provide for the mandatory positions, and ensure the proper funding of the office, pursuant to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 6, Rule 6 of its IRR.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Office had been established.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management submit a Local DRRMF Investment Plan for the current year.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Office prepared and submitted a Local DRRMF Investment Plan and submitted the same to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
a. Remove and replace the major scaling in item 107(PCCP) at station 000+042 to 000+200 (intermittent section) with an area of 67.5 sq.m. and amounting to Php53,285.34;
b. Apply remedial measures on the minor scaling from station 000+200 to 000+060 (intermittent section); and
c. Impose correct liquidated damage to the contractor for the delay of project implementation and deduct the amount from his claims.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management impose and deduct the applicable job order withholding taxes from the wages and compensation of professionals and non-professionals hired on job order basis pursuant to BIR rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management imposed taxes on job order wages in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
a. Send for training the GAD Focal Point and other municipal officials on planning, budgeting and other GAD related activities; and
b. Prepare timely the LGU’s GAD Plan and Budget and the related Plan and Budget Accomplishment Report pursuant to Executive Order No. 273 and JMC No. 2004-001.
observations.managementAction: Some employees were sent to GAD trainings. The LGU prepared its GAD Plan and Budget and the related Plan and Budget Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD. For economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices, employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay Officials, to harmonize GAD related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and other related trainings.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the related recommendation.
observations.reason: (See related finding in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances on Cash Local Treasury to detect and adjust the negative abnormal amount in order that the accurate balance of the account is reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to fully reconcile the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to use the Annual Investment Program as basis for the preparation of the Annual Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 305 (i), R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management consider the following:
a) Strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the utilization of funds to ensure economy and efficiency in the utilization and management of government funds; and
b) Stop the practice of conducting out-of-town trainings/sessions which could be effectively done within the premises of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management did not conduct out-of-town Lakbay Aral in CY 2017 and not conduct out-of-town Lakbay Aral in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management consider the following:
a. Work for the hiring of a qualified applicant for the position of Municipal Treasurer. Fill up the vacant position with the most qualified personnel through wider reach of publication and posting process; and
b. Require the Human Resource Management Officer to adopt innovative ways to attract applicants for the vacant position.
observations.managementAction: Management was on the process of hiring a Municipal Treasurer but was not able to hire one. Partially implemented. Partially implemented (Reiterated in Part II of this report).

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of full funded programs/projects/activities to ensure that the needs of the constituents are met;
b) Advise the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Plan; and
c) Require the MPDC to review regularly the status of implementation of the Annual Investment Plan and submit periodic reports to the Local Chief Executive as his basis in taking immediate appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendations. (Reiterated in Part II of this Report). Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management appropriate funds to replace the stolen materials for the project to be operational. Upon replacement, turnover the project to the incumbent Punong Barangay for custody and maintenance. Further, educate the constituents to value and safeguard projects given to them which could help uplift their living condition.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already retrieved the stolen materials but were not yet installed because there was a problem on who will shoulder the electric bill of the said project.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess PEI granted to officials and employees totaling to P1,440,587.84.
observations.managementAction: PEI disallowances were not collected in full. Management sent a letter to COA Central Office for the lifting the PEI disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to: d) Demand liquidation of past due cash advances from officers and employees concerned; e) Avoid granting cash advances to accountable officers whose previous cash advances are not yet liquidated; and f) Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents.
observations.managementAction: Management did not comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management consider the following:
a) Strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the utilization of funds to ensure economy and efficiency in the utilization and management of government funds; and
b) Stop the practice of conducting out-of-town trainings/sessions which could be effectively done within the premises of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU conducted an out-of-town Lakbay-Aral of its officials and employees in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP for CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management still did not prepare an LDRRMFIP in CY 2016.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess PEI granted to officials and employees totaling to P1,440,587.84.
observations.managementAction: Management filed an appeal with the Regional Director but was denied.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the refund of the amount of P33,600.00 for CY 2011 and P44,800.00 for CY 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for an appeal on the decision of the Regional Director.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management exert extra effort to coordinate with the appropriate government agencies to hasten the reclassification of the vehicle from private to government plate.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted efforts to have the vehicle be registered to government plate, still to no avail.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We have recommended and Management agreed to consider the following
measures:
1.10.1. require officials/employees concerned with unliquidated cash advances to
immediately settle their accounts through submission of liquidation
documents and to refund any excess, otherwise, withholding of salary
and/or other sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with
COA Circular No. 97-002;
1.10.2. suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and
other officials/employees until the their previous cash advance have been
liquidated, to prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances;
and
1.10.3. strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to
ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.

1.5. We have recommended that Management consider the following:
1.5.1.work for the hiring of a qualified applicant for the position of Municipal
Treasurer. Fill up the vacant position with the most qualified personnel
through a wider reach of publication and posting process; and
1.5.2.require the Human Resource Management Officer to adopt innovative
ways to attract applicants for the vacant position.

3.7. We have recommended that Management:
3.7.1.recall the designated ICO-Municipal Treasurer to her regular position as the
Municipal Accountant; and
3.7.2.observe the guidelines on designation in the civil service by designating
personnel with appropriate qualifications.

4.7. We have recommended that Management promptly act and expedite the
enactment of an ordinance for the creation of the MDRRMO, provide for the
mandatory positions, and ensure the proper funding of the office, pursuant to
Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 6, Rule 6 of its IRR.

5.3. We have recommended and Management agreed to submit a Local DRRMF
Investment Plan for the current year.

6.4. We have recommended that Management:
6.4.1.remove and replace the major scaling in item 107(PCCP) at station 000 +
042 to 000 + 200 (intermittent section) with an area of 67.5 sq.m. and
amounting to P53,285.34;
6.4.2.apply remedial measures on the minor scaling from station 000 + 200 to
000 + 260 (intermittent section); and
6.4.3.impose the correct liquidated damage to the contractor for the delay of
project implementation and deduct the amount from his claims.

8.7. We have recommended that Management impose and deduct the applicable
withholding taxes from the wages and compensation of professionals and non-
professionals hired on job order basis pursuant to BIR rules and regulations.

9.3. We have recommended and Management agreed to enforce the settlement of
disallowances.

10.5. We have recommended that Management:
10.5.1. send for training the GAD Focal Point and other municipal officials on
planning, budgeting and other GAD related activities; and
10.5.2. prepare timely the LGU’s GAD Plan and Budget and the related
Accomplishment Report pursuant to Executive Order No. 273 and JMC
No. 2004-01.

11.5. We have recommended and management agreed to require all elected officials
and heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD. For
economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize
the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices,
employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-
related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD
shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive
planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as
the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka
Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines
and other related trainings.

We have reiterated our recommendation and started reconciling the amounts. Management agreed to instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances on Cash Local Treasury to detect and adjust the negative abnormal amount in order that the accurate balance of the account is reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management started reconciling the amounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to form an inventory committee to strictly conduct a complete inventory of all MLGU property and reconcile these with accounting records. Further, demand physical presentation of all property and require refund or replacement of lost or missing items.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160 on the “Use of Appropriated Funds and Savings” by advising the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Budget to follow the procedural requirement to avoid questionable transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to use the Annual Investment Program as the basis for the preparation of the Annual Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 305 (i), R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to discontinue charging expenses that are not related to the implementation of 30% Quick Response Fund and henceforth observe the proper utilization of DRRMF in accordance with prevailing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to work for the hiring of a qualified applicant for the position of Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The MLGU was not able to hire a Municipal Treasurer in CY 2016. Management is in the process of hiring.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of full funded programs/projects/activities to ensure that the needs of the constituents are met;
b) Advise the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Plan; and
c) Require the MPDC to review regularly the status of implementation of the Annual Investment Plan and submit periodic reports to the Local Chief Executive as his basis in taking immediate appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management limit the hiring of JOs for authorized projects only.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to collect P310.00 in CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management appropriate funds to replace the stolen materials for the project to be operational. Upon replacement, turnover the project to the incumbent Punong Barangay for custody and maintenance. Further, educate the constituents to value and safeguard projects given to them which could help uplift their living condition.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already retrieved the stolen materials but were not yet installed because there was a problem on who will shoulder the electric bill of the said project.
